metadata.title: The Madison +1 & Ship It: PDF + Figma
metadata.playlist: Name It & Brand It — Your Strategic Identity
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 6 brief — Brand Strategy + Naming

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 7 of 7. The last step in Assignment 6 has two parts. First: the Madison plus-one — run the recipe pipeline to draft your Mission and UVP, then rewrite both in your own voice and submit both versions with an explanation of what you changed and why. Second: assemble the PDF and update the Figma board. One named pitfall before we go in: students forget to update the Figma board and only submit the PDF. The Figma link is a required separate submission. Let's close it.

props.segment: The Madison +1 & Ship It: PDF + Figma
props.command: Madison +1: pipeline draft + human rewrite + explain what changed. PDF: all parts in order. Figma: update + submit link.
props.runningText: Figma link is a required separate submission — students who forget lose the touchpoint points…
output: Madison +1: pipeline version + human rewrite + 2–3 sentence explanation
PDF: all parts in order (under 30MB, labeled, screenshots labeled)
Figma link in the Canvas comment

beats.narration_text: The Madison plus-one. Run your tool recipe from Assignment 5B — or, if your tool is not yet fully built, run the recipe's pipeline steps using the brand_config.json as the input. The specific outputs you need are a draft Mission statement and a draft UVP, generated by the pipeline. If the recipe includes an AI node that synthesizes from the tool description and the primary user description, those two outputs are what you are looking for. If the pipeline does not generate these directly, use the Claude prompt from Episode 1 with your brand_config.json fields as the input — that counts as running the pipeline for this purpose. Document the pipeline's version — the Mission it generated and the UVP it generated — exactly as the output came out of the AI. Then write your own version of each: a Mission you would say in an interview, a UVP that passes the gut-check test from Episode 1. Submit both versions. Then write two to three sentences explaining what you changed and why — this is the plus-one. Not 'I made it sound better' — specifically: which words you replaced, which specifics you added, and what judgment the pipeline could not make. The pipeline cannot know that the primary user in your context is a first-generation marketing student with no technical background. If you added that specificity, say so. That is the judgment the pipeline cannot supply.

props.artifactHeading: Pipeline draft → human rewrite → explain the judgment
artifactLines: Run the recipe pipeline (or Episode 1 prompt with brand_config fields) → get Mission + UVP draft.
Submit the pipeline version exactly as-is — do not edit it before showing it.
Write your version: Mission + UVP that pass the gut-check test from Episode 1.
Explain in 2–3 sentences: which words you replaced, what specifics you added, what judgment the pipeline couldn't make.
Not 'I made it sound better' — name the specific decision you brought that AI couldn't.
props.sparkLine: Pipeline version + human version + your judgment. Three things, not one.

beats.narration_text: The PDF. Name it with your last name first: LastName_FirstName_A6_BrandNaming.pdf. It must be under thirty megabytes — if your screenshots are making it large, compress them before inserting. The PDF has eight sections in order, each with a visible header. Section 1: Brand Foundation — Mission, Purpose, Vision, Values, and UVP development with all three to five iterations documented. Section 2: Positioning and Pillars — the five pillars with concrete anchors, and the competitive matrix with named comparators and the white space statement. Section 3: Name Generation — all ten names with rationales and scores. Section 4: Trademark Search — the five searches, the table, the screenshots embedded or labeled as appendices, and the first-pass disclaimer. Section 5: URL Availability — the three-by-four table and the final name recommendation with justification. Section 6: Portfolio Analysis — the individual analyses with screenshots and the synthesis. Section 7: Madison +1 — the pipeline version, your version, and the explanation. Section 8: Figma update summary — a screenshot of the updated Figma board with the final name and the one-to-two sentence description visible. Page numbers and labeled headers make the PDF gradeable. A PDF where the grader has to hunt for the competitive matrix costs grading time you do not want to cost.

artifactLines: Section 1: Brand Foundation (Mission/Purpose/Vision/Values + UVP iterations).
Section 2: Positioning + Pillars (5 pillars with anchors + matrix + white space).
Section 3: Name Generation (10 names with rationale + score).
Section 4: Trademark Search (5 searches + table + screenshots + disclaimer).
Section 5: URL Availability (3×4 table + final name recommendation).
Section 6: Portfolio Analysis (5–8 with screenshots + synthesis).
Section 7: Madison +1 (pipeline version + your version + explanation).
Section 8: Figma update screenshot. Under 30MB. Page numbers. Labeled headers.
props.sparkLine: 8 sections. Headers. Page numbers. Under 30MB. Grader finds everything in 30 seconds.

beats.act: the figma update and the final submission
beats.narration_text: The Figma board update. Open the Figma board you have been building since Assignment 2. Find the section for the tool's identity — if you do not have one yet, add a frame. Update three things: the final name from Episode 5, a one-to-two sentence description of the tool using language from your Mission and UVP, and a simplified version of the competitive chart from Episode 2 that shows the tool's position relative to the comparators. The Figma update does not require a full rebrand — it is an update, not a redesign. The three elements signal to the grader that the brand work in the PDF is connected to the tool you have been building. Copy the share link for the Figma board — set it to view-only before copying. In Canvas, submit the PDF file. In the assignment comment field, paste the Figma link. That is the complete submission: PDF attached plus Figma link in the comment. Before you submit, read your final UVP out loud one more time — to someone who has not seen it. If they ask a question or look confused, that sentence is your last revision before you export the PDF. Fix the gap. Then submit.

artifactLines: Figma: final name + 1–2 sentence description (from Mission/UVP) + simplified competitive chart.
Share link: set to view-only before copying.
Canvas: PDF file uploaded + Figma link pasted in the comment field.
Pre-submit: read the UVP aloud to someone who hasn't seen it — fix anything that confuses them.
PDF attached + Figma link in comment = complete submission. Both required.
props.sparkLine: Figma update + PDF + Figma link in comment. Missing the link = incomplete.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for the Madison plus-one and Ship It. The Madison plus-one: run the pipeline to generate a Mission and UVP draft; submit the pipeline version exactly as output; write your version; explain in two to three sentences what you changed and what judgment the pipeline could not make. The PDF: eight sections in order with visible headers, page numbers, screenshots labeled or embedded, and under thirty megabytes. The Figma update: final name, one to two sentence description, simplified competitive chart. In Canvas: PDF attached and Figma link in the comment field. Read your final UVP out loud before you export. If someone who has not seen it is confused, fix that sentence first. Then ship it.

props.artifactTitle: The Madison +1 & Ship It: PDF + Figma
artifactLines: Madison +1: pipeline version + your version + 2–3 sentence explanation (what judgment you added).
PDF: 8 sections · labeled headers · page numbers · screenshots embedded · under 30MB.
Figma: final name + 1–2 sentence description + simplified competitive chart.
Canvas: PDF file + Figma link in the comment field. Both required.
Pre-submit: read UVP aloud. Fix what confuses someone who hasn't seen it. Then ship.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. One prompt for the Madison plus-one, then a checklist before you export. The plus-one prompt reads: I ran my recipe pipeline and received this draft Mission: [paste pipeline output]. And this draft UVP: [paste pipeline output]. I want to write my own versions of both. Here is information the pipeline did not have access to: [two to three sentences about your context — your actual primary user, the specific thing your tool does differently, any personal motivation that is not in the config]. Using this additional context, rewrite the Mission in one sentence and the UVP in one sentence. Then write two to three sentences explaining exactly what was changed from the pipeline version, what specific words were replaced, what context was added, and what judgment call was made that the pipeline could not make. After you get the plus-one draft, run the pre-submit checklist: one, does the PDF have all eight sections with visible headers? Two, are all screenshots labeled? Three, is the PDF under thirty megabytes? Four, is the Figma board updated with the final name, description, and competitive chart? Five, is the Figma link set to view-only? Six, have you read the UVP out loud to someone who has not seen it? If the answer to any of these is no, fix it before you submit. Then submit.

props.segment: The Madison +1 & Ship It: PDF + Figma
props.command: Pipeline Mission draft: [paste]. Pipeline UVP draft: [paste]. Context the pipeline didn't have: [2–3 sentences]. Rewrite Mission + UVP. Explain: what changed · what words replaced · what context added · what judgment the pipeline couldn't make.
props.runningText: checklist before export: 8 sections · screenshots labeled · under 30MB · Figma updated · link view-only · UVP read aloud…

beats.narration_text: The Madison +1 & Ship It: PDF + Figma. Liam, in for Bear.
